content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN positive integers.

Count the number of ordered pairs of **disjoint** non-empty subsequences (S1,S2)(S_1, S_2)(S1‚Äã,S2‚Äã) such that gcd‚Å°(S1)=mex(S2)\gcd(S_1) = \text{mex}(S_2)gcd(S1‚Äã)=mex(S2‚Äã).

Here, the mex of a sequence of integers is the smallest **positive** integer that doesn't appear in it.

Print the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note that:


A subsequence of AAA is a sequence obtained by taking any subset S‚äÜ{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}S\subseteq \{1, 2, \ldots, N\}S‚äÜ{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} and looking at the sequence of elements corresponding to SSS.
Two subsequences are considered different if they are obtained from different index sets, even if the elements in the resulting sequence are equal.

Thus, an array of length NNN has exactly 2N2^N2N subsequences (including the empty sequence).
Two subsequences are considered disjoint if their index sets do not intersect.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid pairs of subsequences, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
The sum of max‚Å°(A)\max(A)max(A) across all tests won't exceed 10610^6106.
